title: copywriting
purpose: Produce clear, credible, action-oriented marketing copy through a phased process of context gathering, brief lock, structured page writing, and testability guidance.
when: When writing new page copy from scratch (homepage, landing page, pricing, feature, about) with a structured phased approach.

title: copy-editing
purpose: Systematically improve existing copy through seven sequential editing sweeps covering clarity, voice, relevance, proof, specificity, emotion, and risk reduction.
when: When reviewing and improving existing marketing or conversion copy, or editing a first draft before publication.

title: journalistic-writing
purpose: Produce newsroom-grade, evidence-aware writing with public-interest structure, attribution discipline, and controlled language.
when: When writing news reports, features, explainers, interviews, analysis, headlines, or publication-style rewrites.

title: marketing-writing
purpose: Produce persuasive, conversion-oriented writing tuned for audience, offer, and action across landing pages, ads, emails, and product copy.
when: When writing landing pages, ads, performance copy, product descriptions, email sequences, slogans, CTA blocks, or campaign messaging.

title: business-writing
purpose: Produce clear, efficient professional communication for email, chat, memos, status reports, proposals, and executive summaries.
when: When writing emails, chat messages, internal updates, partnership outreach, status reports, meeting notes, follow-ups, proposals, or executive summaries.

title: legal-writing
purpose: Produce legally cautious, structured drafting support for clauses, policies, notices, disclaimers, and formal documents with risk awareness.
when: When drafting contract clauses, templates, notices, disclaimers, policies, legal summaries, definitions, or structured obligations and rights.

title: general-writing
purpose: Produce natural, emotionally appropriate everyday text for personal messages, social posts, casual notes, and informal communication.
when: When writing messages to friends and family, personal notes, casual rewrites, social posts, thank-you messages, congratulations, invitations, or everyday captions.

title: language-handling
purpose: Control multilingual input, translation fidelity, code-switching, and target-language consistency across all writing tasks.
when: When input contains multiple languages, user asks for bilingual output, part of source text must remain untranslated, or cultural and tone fidelity matters across languages.
